text: nature portfolio
Reporting Summary

Nature Portfolio wishes to improve the reproducibility of the work that we publish. This form provides structure for consistency and transparency in reporting. For further information on Nature Portfolio policies, see our Editorial Policies and the Editorial Policy Checklist.
Statistics
For all statistical analyses, confirm that the following items are present in the figure legend, table legend, main text, or Methods section.
Our web collection on statistics for biologists contains articles on many of the points above.
Software and code
Policy information about availability of computer code
Data collection

X-ray data were processed using Xia2 0.5.900. Model building were performed with Coot 0.8.9.2 followed by steps of refinement with Phenix 1.17.1-3660. Molecular dynamics simulations were performed using the Amber 2020 software. Substrate entry channels were calculated using Caver 3 software.

ysis Figures and density maps were generated with Pymol 2.3.3, graphs were created with Microsoft Excel. Michaelis-Menten plots were generated with GraphPad Prism 9. Conformational landscapes and histograms of loop distances were created using Matplotlib python librar

For manuscripts utilizing custom algorithms or software that are central to the research but not yet described in published literature, software must be made available to editors and reviewers. We strongly encourage code deposition in a community repository (e.g. GitHub). See the Nature Portfolio guidelines for submitting code & software for further information.
Data
Policy information about availability of data
All manuscripts must include a data availability statement. This statement should provide the following information, where applicable:

- Accession codes, unique identifiers, or web links for publicly available datasets - A description of any restrictions on data availability - For clinical datasets or third party data, please ensure that the statement adheres to our policy
Structure coordinates for all Kemp eliminases have been deposited in the RCSB Protein Data Bank with the following accession codes: 1A53-Core (8FMC [https://
